asset_type: TaraHome technical blog cover, landscape article hero and 16:9 card crop
prompt: A clean editorial photograph that immediately suggests a local smart-home network working inside the house, for an article explaining whether Home Assistant Matter needs IPv6. Show a warm, lived-in residential utility nook or low oak console near a softly lit living room, with a natural plaster wall and real wood grain. Feature an ordinary unbranded home Wi-Fi router with visible Ethernet ports and one connected cable beside a small unbranded local home-automation hub; include a warm lamp and a simple door sensor in the room. Use photorealistic natural editorial interior photography, believable consumer equipment, a wide horizontal eye-level three-quarter composition, warm late-afternoon light, and restrained dark-green accents. Keep the equipment clear in the middle third for both the article hero and centered card crop. No people, text, logos, brand marks, screen interface, QR codes, watermark, named product model, embedded headline, diagrams, arrows, decorative routing or network path overlays, glowing connection trails, translucent panels, inset frames, floating UI cards, progress bars, fake charts, server-room aesthetic, neon sci-fi lighting, or generic AI infographic styling.
